title: Under the Non-Financial Reporting Directive (NFRD) from the European Union (EU), what are large public-interest companie…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Under the Non-Financial Reporting Directive (NFRD) from the European Union (EU), what are large public-interest companies required to disclose?


Reveal how they operate and address social and environmental challenges.

Reveal the financial data of their operations in Europe.

Disclose the steps they take to upskill their employees on ethics and compliance issues.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Disclose their policies, risks and outcomes regarding environmental, social and employee-related matters, respect for human rights, and anti-corruption and bribery issues (including diversity, non-discrimination, and gender equality).

Describe their business model and how it impacts sustainability.

Identify the principal risks and uncertainties they face, and the key performance indicators they use.

Provide information on their supply chain, including how they manage social and environmental risks within it.

Explain their approach to due diligence and how they ensure that their activities do not contribute to adverse human rights impacts.

Provide information on their policy outcomes, targets, and performance.

Provide a description of their board diversity policy, including objectives and results.
